title: lathyrus asiaticus
intro: Lathyrus asiaticus, also known as the Indian pea or lathyrus, is a perennial legume native to the Indian subcontinent and parts of Asia, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and edible seeds. The plant contains a toxin called ODAP (oxalyldiaminopropionic acid), which, when consumed in large amounts, can cause neurolathyrism, a condition characterized by muscle spasms and paralysis. However, in moderation, Lathyrus asiaticus seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus asiaticus, such as decoctions and infusions, can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
